toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: skf-test-skill
description: Cognitive completeness verification — quality gate before export. Use when the user requests to "test a skill" or "verify skill completeness."
---

# Test Skill

## Overview

Verifies that a skill is complete enough to be useful to an AI agent by checking coverage of the public API surface (naive mode) or validating SKILL.md + references coherence (contextual mode). Produces a completeness score and gap report as a quality gate before export. Every finding must trace to actual code with file:line citations.

## Conventions

- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.

## Role

You are a skill auditor and completeness analyst operating in Ferris's Audit mode. This is a deterministic quality gate — you bring AST-backed analysis expertise and zero-hallucination verification, while the skill artifacts provide the evidence.

## Workflow Rules

These rules apply to every step in this workflow:

- Zero hallucination — every finding must trace to actual code with file:line citations
- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
- Update `stepsCompleted` in output file frontmatter before loading next step
- Always communicate in `{communication_language}`
- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with their default action and log each auto-decision

## Stages

| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed |
|---|------|------|--------------|
| 1 | Initialize & Load Skill | references/init.md | Yes |
| 2 | Detect Mode | references/detect-mode.md | Yes |
| 3 | Coverage Check | references/coverage-check.md | Yes |
| 4 | Coherence Check | references/coherence-check.md | Yes |
| 4b | External Validators | references/external-validators.md | Yes |
| 5 | Score | references/score.md | Yes |
| 6 | Report | references/report.md | No (confirm) |
| 7 | Workflow Health Check | references/health-check.md | Yes |

## Invocation Contract

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| **Inputs** | skill_name [required]; optional flags: `--allow-workspace-drift`, `--no-discovery` (skip §4b Discovery Testing), `--no-health-check` (skip §7 health-check dispatch), `--tier=<Quick\|Forge\|Forge+\|Deep>` (bypass forge-tier.yaml sidecar requirement), `--threshold=<N>` (override pass threshold; CLI wins over `workflow.default_threshold` scalar) |
| **Gates** | step 6: Confirm Gate [C] |
| **Outputs** | test-report-{skill_name}.md with completeness score and result (PASS/FAIL); per-run `skf-test-skill-result-{run_id}.json` and `skf-test-skill-result-latest.json` written atomically under `{forge_version}/` |
| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with default action when `{headless_mode}` is true |
| **Exit codes** | See "Exit Codes" below |

## Exit Codes

Every terminal state in this workflow exits with a stable code so headless automators can branch on the verdict (and any HARD HALT) without grepping message text:

| Code | Meaning              | Raised by                                                                                  |
| ---- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 0    | success / PASS       | step 6 §6b — `testResult: 'pass'` (after the result contract is written in §4c)            |
| 2    | fail / FAIL          | step 6 §6b — `testResult: 'fail'` (after the result contract is written in §4c)            |
| 3    | inconclusive         | step 6 §6b — `testResult: 'inconclusive'` (distinct from fail so orchestrators can route to manual-review queues) |
| 4    | pass-with-drift      | step 6 §6b — `testResult: 'pass-with-drift'` (distinct from clean pass — `--allow-workspace-drift` was in effect; orchestrators MUST route to re-test-against-pinned-commit queues and refuse export; never exit 0 under drift override) |

## Result Contract (Headless)

When `{headless_mode}` is true, step 6 emits a single-line JSON envelope on **stdout** before chaining to step 7, and every HARD HALT emits the same envelope shape on **stderr** with `status: "error"`:

```
SKF_TEST_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success|error","skill_name":"…","verdict":"PASS|FAIL|INCONCLUSIVE|pass-with-drift","score":N,"threshold":N,"report_path":"…|null","next_workflow":"export-skill|update-skill|null","exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null}
```

`status` is `"success"` on the terminal happy path (any of PASS / FAIL / INCONCLUSIVE / pass-with-drift — the workflow completed), `"error"` on any HARD HALT before §4c. `verdict` is the canonical result string. `next_workflow` is `"export-skill"` only when `verdict == "PASS"`; `"update-skill"` for `FAIL` or `pass-with-drift`; `null` for `INCONCLUSIVE` (manual review). `halt_reason` is `null` on the terminal path or one of the workflow-defined halt strings (`"forge-tier-missing"`, `"target-inaccessible"`, `"write-failed"`, `"step-completeness-violation"`, `"report-anchor-missing"`, `"health-check-missing"`, `"atomic-writer-missing"`). `exit_code` matches the table above.

The same payload is persisted to disk by step 6 §4c (atomic write) at two locations under `{forge_version}/`:

| Path                                          | Purpose                                                                    |
| --------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `skf-test-skill-result-{run_id}.json`         | Per-run record. `{run_id}` carries UTC timestamp + PID + random suffix.    |
| `skf-test-skill-result-latest.json`           | Latest copy — stable path for pipeline consumers (copy, not symlink).      |

The on-disk payload is the richer form: it adds `outputs[]` (report-path entries), `summary` (`score`, `threshold`, `result`, `testMode`, `activeCategories[]`, `inconclusiveReasons[]` when present), `runId`, and `healthCheckDispatched`. The stdout envelope is the compact subset documented above.

## On Activation

1. Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and resolve:
   - `project_name`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`
   - `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`, `sidecar_path`

2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in preferences.yaml. Default: false.

3. **Resolve workflow customization.** Run:

   ```bash
   python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py \
       --skill {skill-root} --key workflow
   ```

   The script merges the three customization layers per `bmad-customize`'s structural merge rules (scalars override, arrays append):

   - `{skill-root}/customize.toml` — bundled defaults
   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.toml` under `{project-root}` — team overrides (committed)
   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.user.toml` under `{project-root}` — personal overrides (gitignored)

   If the script fails or is missing, fall back to reading `{skill-root}/customize.toml` directly — the bundled defaults are an empty string for each path scalar.

   Apply the path-scalar fallback now so stage files don't have to repeat the conditional logic. For each of the three scalars, if the merged value is empty or absent, use the bundled default:

   - `{testReportTemplatePath}` ← `workflow.test_report_template_path` if non-empty, else `templates/test-report-template.md`
   - `{outputFormatsPath}` ← `workflow.output_formats_path` if non-empty, else `assets/output-section-formats.md`
   - `{scoringRulesPath}` ← `workflow.scoring_rules_path` if non-empty, else `references/scoring-rules.md`
   - `{defaultThreshold}` ← `workflow.default_threshold` if non-empty/non-null, else `80`. CLI `--threshold=<N>` (when present) wins over the scalar at the usage site in `references/score.md`.
   - `{onCompleteCommand}` ← `workflow.on_complete` if non-empty, else empty string. When empty, the post-finalization hook in `references/report.md` is a no-op.

   Stash all five as workflow-context variables. Stage files reference `{testReportTemplatePath}` / `{outputFormatsPath}` / `{scoringRulesPath}` / `{defaultThreshold}` / `{onCompleteCommand}` directly — no conditional at the usage site. Empty-string overrides cleanly fall through to the bundled default; non-empty values let orgs swap in house-style copies without forking the skill.

4. Load, read the full file, and then execute `references/init.md` to begin the workflow.

lines: - `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.

+- Step files use `## STEP GOAL:` headings rather than the `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` + `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` pattern used by generate-driven SKF workflows — this skill is a validation harness with score-driven step semantics, not a chain that produces new artifacts.
## Role

input.command:
python3 <<'PYEOF'
import re
import pathlib

pattern = re.compile(r'\*\*CRITICAL:\*\* Follow this sequence exactly\.[^\n]*\n(\n)?', re.MULTILINE)

count_total = 0
for f in pathlib.Path('src').rglob('*.md'):
    if '.analysis' in str(f) or 'references' not in str(f):
        continue
    content = f.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    matches = pattern.findall(content)
    if matches:
        new_content = pattern.sub('', content)
        f.write_text(new_content, encoding='utf-8')
        count_total += len(matches)
        print(f"  {f}: {len(matches)} removed")

print(f"\nTotal: {count_total} additional removed")
echo_remaining=count_total
PYEOF
echo ""
echo "=== Final verify: should be ZERO ==="
grep -r "\*\*CRITICAL:\*\* Follow this sequence" src/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v ".analysis" | wc -l

chore(skf): propagate v2 Workflow Rules trim across skf-* skills

Propagates the per-rule trim from skf-setup's v2 quality pass (commit
3dd864cd) across all 13 SKF workflow skills, plus drops the restated
`**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly...` banner line from step
files. Load-bearing content preserved: `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` headings
(94 instances) and `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` blocks (52
instances) remain intact as step-transition gates.

SKILL.md Workflow Rules — per-rule decision:
- Drop "Read each step file completely..." (restated baseline)
- Drop "Follow the mandatory sequence exactly..." (restated by chain
  mechanism + Stages table)
- Keep "Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps"
  (context-window management — not restated elsewhere; this is the
  deliberate keep from skf-setup's v2 quality pass)

Drift fixes surfaced by the audit:
- Add Rule 3 to skf-quick-skill (was missing — drift from pre-v2-trim)
- Document skf-test-skill's alternative step heading convention
  (## STEP GOAL: instead of ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE — validation harness
  with score-driven semantics, not generate-driven chain)

Step files (69 files across 13 skills):
- Drop "**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder,
  or improvise..." body line — restated by the ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
  heading + Stages table + nextStepFile chain mechanism

Verification: 1346 Python tests pass; 0 broken references; ESLint,
markdownlint, Prettier all clean.

Refs #335 (addresses A4, D1, D2, skf-test-skill subitems; C1/C2/C4
deferred per their per-workstream triggers)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "chore(skf): propagate v2 Workflow Rules trim across skf-* skills" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Propagates the per-rule Workflow Rules trim from skf-setup's v2 quality pass (commit 3dd864cd) across all 13 SKF workflow skills, plus drops the restated `**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly...` ritual line from step files.
- Surfaces and fixes two drifts during the audit: missing Rule 3 in `skf-quick-skill`, and undocumented alternative step heading convention in `skf-test-skill`.
- Load-bearing content preserved: 94 `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` headings, 52 `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` blocks, and all `Auto-Proceed` inline behavioral instructions remain intact.

## What changed

### SKILL.md Workflow Rules — per-rule decision (13 files)

- **Drop** "Read each step file completely..." — restated baseline
- **Drop** "Follow the mandatory sequence in each step exactly..." — restated by the chain mechanism + Stages table
- **Keep** "Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps" — context-window management; not restated elsewhere. This is the deliberate keep from the v2 skf-setup pass; this PR brings the other 12 workflow skills into alignment.

### Drift fixes surfaced by the audit

- `skf-quick-skill`: was missing Rule 3 entirely (drift from before the v2 trim convention existed) — Rule 3 added.
- `skf-test-skill`: documents its alternative step heading convention. Step files use `## STEP GOAL:` instead of `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` because this skill is a validation harness with score-driven semantics, not a generate-driven chain. The note prevents future contributors from "fixing" the intentional deviation.

### Step files (69 files across 13 skills)

- **Drop** the restated `**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise...` ritual line. The information is restated by the `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` heading + the Stages table + the `nextStepFile` chain mechanism — three independent mechanical enforcement paths.

### What this PR does NOT change

- `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` headings (structural — they organize numbered substeps)
- `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` blocks (load-bearing — these are the step-transition gates declaring "ONLY WHEN X has happened will you load `{nextStepFile}`")
- `Auto-Proceed` mentions (inline behavioral instructions for headless mode)
- `skf-setup` SKILL.md (already correct per v2 commit 3dd864cd)
- `skf-forger` SKILL.md (non-workflow agent persona — correctly excluded)

## Diff stats

82 files changed, 2 insertions(+), 162 deletions(-)

## Test plan

- [x] All 1346 Python tests pass
- [x] 0 broken file references
- [x] 0 absolute path leaks
- [x] ESLint clean
- [x] markdownlint clean across 224 markdown files
- [x] Prettier formatting clean
- [ ] Sanity-check: open `skf-drop-skill/SKILL.md` and confirm the Workflow Rules section now lists Rule 3 alongside skill-specific rules, with no Rule 1/Rule 2 residue
- [ ] Sanity-check: open `skf-drop-skill/references/select.md` and confirm `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` heading remains, the `**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence...` ritual line is gone, and `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` body is intact

Refs #335 — addresses A4 (already superseded), D1 (Workflow Rules trim), D2 (step-file CRITICAL ritual line), and the `skf-test-skill` documentation item. C1, C2, C4 remain open per their per-workstream demand-gated triggers.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
**PR #336 opened: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/336**

## What landed

- **Commit** `08c01c98` on branch `chore/skf-issue-335-drop-redundant-scaffolding`
- **82 files changed, 2 insertions, 162 deletions**
- Pre-commit hooks passed full validation: 1346 Python tests, schema + reference checks, ESLint, markdownlint (224 .md), Prettier

## What was avoided (regression prevention)

The audit caught that the v2 skf-setup quality pass (commit `3dd864cd`) made a deliberate **per-rule** decision — keep Rule 3, drop Rules 1+2. The original #335 framing of "drop the whole trio" would have undone that. The corrected plan:
- Propagates the v2 per-rule trim module-wide
- Surfaces and fixes a real drift (`skf-quick-skill` missing Rule 3)
- Documents `skf-test-skill`'s intentional alternative convention
- Preserves all load-bearing content (94 MANDATORY SEQUENCE headings + 52 CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE gates + Auto-Proceed inline instructions)

## What's still open on #335

- **C1** (EX distribution templates): demand-gated, no action needed until external user asks
- **C2** (DS resume after `[N]` cancel): needs its own design discussion issue
- **C4** (RS source-authority audit): demand-gated, no action needed until compliance adopter asks

input.content:
---
name: verify-deferred-issue-framings
description: Tracking issues that sit open accumulate stale claims; verify against current code before bulk-applying any cleanup the issue prescribes
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

When closing out a deferred or parked tracking issue, verify every load-bearing claim in the issue body against current code state before executing the prescribed action. Issues that sit open accumulate staleness — claims that were true at filing time may have been superseded by intermediate work the issue didn't track.

**Why:** Issue #335 (SKF Architecture/Utility/Management deferrals) framed the D1 trio cleanup as "drop all three Workflow Rules bullets across 12 SKILL.md files." But the v2 skf-setup quality pass (commit 3dd864cd) had already made a deliberate **per-rule** decision: drop Rules 1+2 as restated baseline, *keep* Rule 3 for context-window management. The issue's framing predated that decision and would have undone it if bulk-applied. Two other stale claims in the same issue: D2's "brief-skill is simpler auto-proceed style; update-skill is stricter" (both carried the same pattern by the time the cleanup ran) and D2's "drop three banner types" (Auto-Proceed wasn't a banner — it was inline behavioral content).

**How to apply:** Before any bulk action prescribed by a deferred issue:
1. Re-grep the artifacts the issue references — counts, file lists, conventions
2. Read the most recent commits that touched the relevant files (`git log -- <paths>`) to surface intermediate quality decisions
3. Use claude-mem to search for prior reasoning about the artifacts
4. Treat issue framings as hypotheses to verify, not specs to execute

Especially load-bearing when the issue uses words like "drop everywhere," "ratify everywhere," or "whole-module decision" — those framings are exactly where per-item nuance gets lost.

Related: [[project_skf_workflow_rules_convention]]

input.content:
---
name: skf-workflow-rules-convention
description: SKF Workflow Rules in SKILL.md use a per-rule convention — Rule 3 (no preload) is module-wide policy; Rules 1+2 were dropped as restated baseline in PR #336
metadata:
  type: project
---

SKF workflow skills carry a single bullet under `## Workflow Rules` in SKILL.md from the original "trio" pattern: **"Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps."** This is module-wide policy as of PR #336 (commit 08c01c98 → merged to v2 as 811c8fff, 2026-05-16).

The previous "trio" (Read each step file completely + Follow the mandatory sequence exactly + Only load one step file at a time) was reduced to just Rule 3 because:
- Rule 1 ("read completely") is restated baseline behavior for any LLM working from instructions
- Rule 2 ("follow sequence") is restated by the `nextStepFile` chain mechanism + Stages table
- Rule 3 ("only load one") is **not** restated elsewhere — it's the only rule that actively prevents an LLM failure mode (premature loading of future step files), which costs context budget and biases the model toward unnecessary content

**Why:** The v2 skf-setup quality pass (commit 3dd864cd) introduced this per-rule decision. PR #336 propagated it to the 12 other workflow skills, fixed drift in `skf-quick-skill` (which was missing Rule 3 entirely from before the v2 pass), and documented `skf-test-skill`'s alternative step heading convention (`## STEP GOAL:` instead of `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` — validation harness with score-driven semantics, not a generate-driven chain).

**How to apply:**
- New workflow skills authored after 2026-05-16 should carry only Rule 3 in Workflow Rules, plus any skill-specific rules
- Do NOT add Rules 1 or 2 back — they're explicitly redundant
- Do NOT remove Rule 3 — it's the load-bearing exception
- `skf-forger` is the non-workflow exception (agent persona, no chain mechanics) — no Workflow Rules section at all
- Step files preserve `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` heading (structural) and `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` blocks (load-bearing step-transition gates) but should NOT carry the redundant `**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly...` ritual line

Related: [[feedback_verify_deferred_issue_framings]]

1	- [Issue refs in commits/PRs](feedback_no_issue_numbers_in_pr.md) — Reference same-repo GitHub issues (`Fixes #NNN`); never reference internal `_bmad-output/todo/` IDs
2	- [OSS commit/PR hygiene](feedback_oss_commit_hygiene.md) — Public-facing text (commits, PRs, comments) never references internal artifacts (gitignored paths, BMad analysis reports, decision logs, runtime state dirs)
3	- [CHANGELOG.md is release-managed](feedback_changelog_managed_by_release.md) — Never hand-edit CHANGELOG.md; it's regenerated by the release workflow from commits/PRs
4	- [No inline version-stamp markers in user docs](feedback_no_inline_version_stamps_in_docs.md) — Don't write `(vX.Y.Z+)` / "Added in vX.Y.Z" inline in `docs/`/`README`; CHANGELOG is the canonical "when added" record
5	- [SKF docs link out, don't duplicate external ecosystems](feedback_skf_docs_link_out_not_duplicate.md) — Link to canonical external docs (e.g. docs.bmad-method.org); SKF docs stay focused on SKF's unique integration value
6	- [skf-test-skill improvement plan](project_skf_test_skill_improvement_plan.md) — Prioritized quality improvements: frontmatter script, Path to PASS, handoff validation, parallelism; boilerplate surgery parked
7	- [Layer 2 tenant candidates research](reference_skf_layer2_candidates_research.md) — Full evaluation of repowise/graphify/CodeGraphContext/GitNexus; drove ROADMAP.md Layer 2 Tenant Candidates section (2026-04-19)
8	- [SKF Workspace roadmap](project_skf_workspace_roadmap.md) — Persistent workspace at ~/.skf/workspace/, Layer 0 minimal, Layers 1-3 triggered by usage signals
9	- [SKF Buy Me a Coffee link](reference_skf_support_link.md) — https://buymeacoffee.com/armelhbobdad — not in repo/FUNDING.yml, only here
10	- [Release pipeline needs E2E staging validation](feedback_release_pipeline_e2e_validation.md) — Story 5.2 took 7 attempts + ~6h, surfacing 5 latent defects because the main-dispatch path had zero prior E2E coverage
11	- [release.yaml shell/gh gotchas](feedback_release_yaml_shell_gotchas.md) — iterate jq arrays via `while IFS= read`, not `for VAR in $(...)`; `gh pr view --json merged` is invalid (use `state`)
12	- [test:python must include new Python test files](feedback_test_python_must_include_new_suites.md) — package.json's test:python enumerates every test path; adding a test/test-skf-*.py file without updating it leaves it dormant in CI
13	- [Cross-platform path assertions](feedback_cross_platform_path_assertions.md) — Tests and JSON-output production code must use Path.as_posix() not str(Path); Windows backslash separators break literal-string comparisons (hit twice — PR #311 and #316)
14	- [v1.0.0 release current status](project_v1_release_status.md) — v1.0.0 LIVE + VERIFIED; Epic 5 CLOSED; Epic 6 stories 6.1/6.2/6.3 all merged — repo has no NPM_TOKEN, no legacy workflows, no release:* scripts; epic-6 awaits code-review of 6.3 to flip → done

content.text:
Two memories saved. Want me to post a status comment on #335 to record which subitems are now closed (so the next visitor doesn't re-investigate the addressed ones)? Draft:

> **PR #336 closes the actionable surface of this issue.**
>
> **Addressed:**
> - **A4** — confirmed superseded (helper-primary refactor already in place)

content.text:
**Post the comment, then stop.**

Three reasons:

1. **It finishes the loop you opened this session with.** The original ask was "genuinely address #335 without regression." Tracker hygiene is the final piece — without the comment, the next visitor wastes time re-investigating A4/D1/D2/test-skill subitems we already settled.

2. **It's low-effort, high-signal.** The draft is ready. The comment converts #335 from "tracking issue with 7 workstreams" into "tracking issue with 3 demand-gated parked items + one I should lift to its own thread." That's the durable closeout.

3. **The natural next thread (chain-reachability lint) is a separate scope.** Amelia's `test:python` safety-net proposal would be valuable but it's a feature PR, not housekeeping. Starting it here would extend a long session into yet-more work when the merged PR is a clean stopping point. Better to fork that as its own session when you're ready.

After the comment posts, the loop is closed. C1/C4 are wontfix-pending-demand and can sit indefinitely. C2 you'll lift to a design issue when you're ready (or I can do that next, your call).

Shall I post?

content.text:
Repo state is quiet — zero open PRs, one open issue (#335, just updated). Here are the natural options:

**Smallest finishing moves (5-10 min each)**
- **Lift C2 to its own design issue** and close #335 (or label C1/C4 as `wontfix-pending-demand` and leave #335 as a parked-work index — your preference)

**Higher-leverage follow-up (1-2 hr)**
- **Build the chain-reachability lint** (Amelia's safety-net from the party deliberation). Adds a `test:python` assertion that every `references/*.md` is reachable from `SKILL.md` via the `nextStepFile` chain. This is the actual mechanical enforcement that the dropped Rules 1+2 only *claimed* to provide — locks in no-regression insurance for the cleanup we just merged.

**Strategic (separate session)**
- **Plan v2 → main merge.** v2 is ~8 commits ahead of main. Not urgent, but it's accumulating.

**Or stop.** You've been working hard this session — closing PR #336 + #335 status comment is a clean wrap point.

My recommendation: if you have energy, the **chain-reachability lint** is the highest-leverage follow-up because it's the only one that adds *new* safety to the cleanup we just landed. If you're done, the smallest finishing move (lift C2) takes 5 minutes and closes the tracker cleanly. Either is defensible.

for f in src/skf-create-skill/references/sub/*.md; do

echo ""
echo "=== Reference docs (no nextStepFile — static references) — count ==="
for skill in src/skf-*/references; do
  total=$(ls "$skill"/*.md 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  with_next=$(grep -l "^nextStepFile:" "$skill"/*.md 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  static=$((total - with_next))
  if [ "$static" -gt "0" ]; then
    echo "$skill: $static static reference docs (no nextStepFile)"
  fi
done | head -20

<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->

# Batch Mode

When `--batch <file>` is supplied, quick-skill processes a list of targets from a text file in sequence rather than a single target from arguments. Designed for unattended bulk runs (CI pipelines, mass-rebuilds, the skf-batch-skills meta-workflow when it lands).

## Input format

One target per line. Empty lines and lines starting with `#` (after optional leading whitespace) are ignored. Each non-empty line has the same shape as the single-target `target` argument, with optional space-separated per-line modifiers:

```
# A batch input file.
lodash
@vercel/og
cognee@0.5.0
https://github.com/foo/bar
https://github.com/foo/bar@2.1.0-beta

# Per-line modifiers — overrides for THIS target only:
lodash language=javascript scope=src/
cognee@0.5.0 language=python scope=cognee/api/
```

Recognised per-line modifiers:

| Modifier | Effect (this target only) |

content.text: This is an architecturally valid orphan, not a bug. `batch-mode.md` is a **conditional entry point** loaded from SKILL.md's On Activation section (when `--batch` is set), not the Stages table. The lint should treat any `references/...md` file mentioned anywhere in SKILL.md as a valid chain entry, not just Stages-table entries. Let me fix the test.

input.old_string:
"""Chain reachability lint for SKF workflow skills.

For each `src/skf-*/` workflow skill, asserts:

1. Every step-file path listed in SKILL.md's Stages table exists on disk.
2. Every `nextStepFile` value in step-file frontmatter resolves to an existing
   file (or to a recognised external target such as `shared/health-check.md`).
3. Every step file (anything with a `nextStepFile` frontmatter key, plus
   anything explicitly named in the Stages table) is reachable from the
   Stages-table entries by walking the `nextStepFile` chain.

The third check is the safety net: it catches step files that exist on disk
but no chain reaches — the failure mode the dropped "Workflow Rules" trio
only claimed to prevent. `skf-forger` is excluded because it is an agent
persona, not a chained workflow.
"""
input.new_string:
"""Chain reachability lint for SKF workflow skills.

3. Every step file (anything with a `nextStepFile` frontmatter key) is
   reachable from the SKILL.md entry set by walking the `nextStepFile` chain.

The entry set is every `references/...md` path SKILL.md mentions — the
Stages table for the main flow, plus conditional entries invoked from
On Activation (e.g. `--batch` loading `references/batch-mode.md` in
skf-quick-skill before the main pipeline starts).

The third check is the safety net: it catches step files that exist on disk
but no chain reaches — the failure mode the dropped "Workflow Rules" trio
only claimed to prevent. `skf-forger` is excluded because it is an agent
persona, not a chained workflow.
"""

@pytest.mark.parametrize("skill", WORKFLOW_SKILLS)
def test_step_files_reachable_from_skill_md(
    skill: str, skills: dict[str, dict]
) -> None:
    """Every step file must be reachable from a SKILL.md entry via the nextStepFile chain.

    Entry set is every `references/...md` path SKILL.md mentions: Stages-table
    rows plus conditional entries from On Activation (e.g. `--batch` modes).
    """
    info = skills[skill]
    if not info:
        pytest.skip(f"{skill}: no SKILL.md or references/")
    skill_dir = info["skill_dir"]

    reachable: set[pathlib.Path] = set()
    queue: deque[pathlib.Path] = deque()
    for entry in info["skill_md_entries"]:
        path = (skill_dir / entry).resolve()
        if path.exists() and path not in reachable:
            reachable.add(path)
            queue.append(path)

    while queue:
        current = queue.popleft()
        nx = _next_step(current)
        if nx is None or nx in EXTERNAL_TARGETS:
            continue
        next_path = _resolve_next(current, nx)
        if next_path is None or not next_path.exists() or next_path in reachable:
            continue
        reachable.add(next_path)
        queue.append(next_path)

    orphans = [
        step.relative_to(skill_dir).as_posix()
        for step in info["step_files"]
        if step.resolve() not in reachable
    ]
    assert not orphans, (
        f"{skill}: step files not reachable from SKILL.md entries: {orphans}"
    )

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(skf): add chain-reachability lint for workflow skills

Adds `test/test-skf-chain-reachability.py` — 42 parametrized assertions
(14 workflow skills × 3 checks) that lock in the chain integrity which
the dropped Workflow Rules trio (PR #336) only claimed to provide. The
chain mechanism is now mechanically enforced.

Per workflow skill (every `src/skf-*/` except `skf-forger`):

1. Every step-file path listed in SKILL.md's `## Stages` table exists
   on disk.
2. Every `nextStepFile` value in step-file frontmatter resolves to an
   existing file, or to a recognised external target
   (`shared/health-check.md`, which resolves from the SKF module root).
3. Every step file (anything with a `nextStepFile` frontmatter key) is
   reachable from a SKILL.md entry via the chain. Entry set is every
   `references/...md` path SKILL.md mentions — the Stages table for the
   main flow, plus conditional entries invoked from On Activation
   (e.g. `--batch` loading `references/batch-mode.md` in skf-quick-skill
   before the main pipeline starts).

Handles `../parent.md` paths used by sub-flow steps (e.g.
`references/sub/ccc-discover.md` chains back to `references/extract.md`)
and the `shared/health-check.md` external target convention.

Wired into `test:python` per the repo convention that the suite must
enumerate every new test path explicitly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
